title: Class 2 scanning input mode — triple_hit emergency shortcut
description: Deterministic scenario for the scanning interaction model when the user signals an emergency mid-loop. Before the per-option iteration completes, the user publishes a button event with event_code='triple_hit'. The scan_input_adapter (PR #108) maps triple_hit to the first CLASS_0 candidate via the emergency shortcut, the manager terminates immediately with submit_selection on that candidate, and the caregiver Phase 2 is bypassed entirely (emergency intent is unambiguous). Reaction time matches direct_select emergency handling — scanning does NOT add latency to the emergency path. Scenario asset only — does not authorize actuation.

steps.description: Publish an insufficient-context payload (button single_click on an unknown event_code, etc.) that drives the policy router into Class 2. The runner attaches routing_metadata.class2_input_mode='scanning'.
Class2ClarificationManager.start_session(input_mode='scanning') initialises the session and begins the per-option loop with announce_class2_scanning_start.

At any point during the scanning loop (option 0 announcement, mid-loop, or before an option completes), the user publishes a button event with event_code='triple_hit'. _try_handle_as_user_selection routes to the scanning branch and calls interpret_button_event_for_scan; the adapter returns a DECISION_EMERGENCY with the first CLASS_0-targeted candidate_id. entry['scan_decision']=('emergency', cand_id) and the per-option event is signaled.

_await_user_scanning_then_caregiver detects the 'emergency' decision in entry, removes the session from _pending_user_class2, and calls submit_selection on the CLASS_0 candidate via the standard terminal pipeline. announce_class2_selection delivers TTS feedback. The remaining options in the scanning loop are NOT announced (emergency shortcut bypasses them).

Publish the terminal clarification record on safe_deferral/clarification/interaction. transition_target='CLASS_0', selection_source='user_mqtt_button'. scan_history may contain entries for any options that completed before the triple_hit arrived (typically just option 0).

_execute_class2_transition routes the CLASS_0 result to the emergency handler (announce_emergency + caregiver notification). No caregiver Phase 2 wait — emergency intent is unambiguous and handled immediately.

Observe the audit stream and capture: scanning preamble, partial scan_history, terminal CLASS_0 transition, emergency announcement. The integration verifier asserts (a) transition_target='CLASS_0', (b) selection_source='user_mqtt_button' (not caregiver), (c) caregiver Phase 2 was NOT invoked.

transition_outcomes.condition: User publishes triple_hit during the scanning loop. The emergency shortcut maps to the first CLASS_0-targeted candidate immediately.

notes: Emergency shortcut preserves direct_select semantics under scanning — the user does not have to wait through N per-option timeouts when an emergency is happening.

scan_input_adapter (PR #108) maps event_code='triple_hit' to DECISION_EMERGENCY with the first CLASS_0-targeted candidate. If the candidate set has no CLASS_0 option (e.g., C208 doorlock-sensitive), the adapter returns DECISION_IGNORE and the loop continues.

caregiver Phase 2 is intentionally NOT invoked — emergency intent is unambiguous and the runtime emits the standard CLASS_0 emergency handling path immediately (announce_emergency + caregiver notification).
scan_history in the terminal record reflects whatever turns completed before the triple_hit arrived (typically just option 0 or empty).
canonical_emergency_family='E001' is illustrative — the actual family depends on which CLASS_0 candidate the manager picked from candidate_choices.
